report_name: AI Agent 'Skills' Pose Major Supply Chain Risk; New Audit Tool Finds 80% Deviate from Declared Behavior

threat_actors.name: AI Supply Chain Attacker
threat_actors.description: A class of threat actor that exploits the AI agent ecosystem by publishing malicious 'skills' to public registries to achieve credential theft, data exfiltration, and remote code execution in enterprise environments.
threat_actors.goals: Credential theft, data exfiltration, and remote code execution within enterprise environments through compromised AI agents.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors publish malicious third-party 'skills' to public registries like OpenClaw, which are then installed by enterprises into their AI agents, compromising the software supply chain.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: A multi-stage attack pattern identified in malicious AI skills, where a skill reads sensitive configuration files or credentials from password stores and then exfiltrates them.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Malicious AI skills exfiltrate stolen credentials and data over the network, often using the agent's existing network permissions in a 'Read-Then-Send' pattern.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: A 'Write-Then-Execute' pattern where a malicious AI skill writes code to a local file and then persuades the LLM agent to execute it, leading to remote code execution via a shell or script interpreter.

attack_patterns.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
attack_patterns.description: Malicious AI skills leverage the inherent, often privileged, permissions of the AI agent to perform actions beyond their stated purpose, constituting privilege abuse.

malware.name: Malicious AI Skill
malware.description: A class of malicious third-party code modules for AI agents, distributed via public registries like OpenClaw. These skills contain hidden, multi-stage attack chains for credential theft, data exfiltration, and remote code execution.

identities.name: OpenClaw
identities.description: A public AI agent skill registry that was analyzed by Unit 42, where 80% of skills were found to deviate from their declared behavior and 5% contained malicious attack chains.

identities.name: Enterprises using AI Agents
identities.description: Organizations that adopt Large Language Model (LLM) agents and extend them with third-party 'skills', making them vulnerable to AI supply chain attacks.

infrastructure.name: Public AI Skill Registries
infrastructure.description: Public registries, such as OpenClaw, used to host and distribute third-party AI agent 'skills', which were found to contain malicious, multi-stage attack chains.

indicators.name: AI Agent Accessing Sensitive Credential Files
indicators.pattern:
title: AI Agent Accessing Sensitive Credential Files
id: 2b1c8d7e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects common AI agent processes (python, node) accessing sensitive credential files.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/ai-agent-supply-chain-risks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1555
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection_process:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/python'
      - '/node'
  selection_file:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '/.aws/credentials'
      - '/.ssh/'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate developer or administrative scripts accessing these files.
indicators.description: Detects AI agent processes accessing sensitive files or directories, such as ~/.aws/ or ~/.ssh/, which could indicate credential theft by a malicious skill.

indicators.name: AI Agent Spawning Shell
indicators.pattern:
title: AI Agent Spawning Shell
id: 9e8f7d6c-5b4a-4c3d-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects common AI agent processes (python, node) spawning a shell, which could indicate remote code execution by a malicious skill.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/ai-agent-supply-chain-risks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1059
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\python.exe'
      - '\node.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate automation scripts that use agents to execute shell commands.
indicators.description: Detects an AI agent process spawning a shell or script interpreter (sh, bash, powershell.exe, cmd.exe), indicative of a 'Write-Then-Execute' RCE attack.

indicators.name: Python Reverse Shell Command Line
indicators.pattern:
title: Python Reverse Shell Command Line
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a one-line Python reverse shell, often used for remote code execution.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/ai-agent-supply-chain-risks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1059.006
  - attack.t1059
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/python'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'socket.socket'
      - '.connect('
      - 'os.dup2'
      - 'pty.spawn'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, but could be triggered by security testing tools.
indicators.description: Detects a classic Python one-liner reverse shell, a technique that could be used by a malicious AI skill to establish a C2 channel.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

relationships.source_name: AI Agent Accessing Sensitive Credential Files
